name: FC Pyunik
content:
FC Pyunik is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia, founded in 1992. It was originally created under the name Homenetmen Yerevan and was renamed Pyunik in 1995, with “Pyunik” meaning “Phoenix,” symbolizing rebirth and resilience. The club quickly established itself in Armenian football by winning the inaugural Armenian Premier League title in 1992 and sharing the championship with Shirak due to equal points.

Throughout the late 1990s and especially the 2000s, Pyunik became the dominant force in Armenian football. After a brief financial crisis and restructuring at the end of the 1990s, the club returned strongly and went on to win multiple league titles, including a remarkable streak of consecutive championships in the early 2000s. During this period, Pyunik also became known for developing many of Armenia’s top players and regularly competing in UEFA competitions, gaining valuable European experience.

In the 2010s and 2020s, Pyunik continued to be one of Armenia’s leading clubs domestically while also achieving notable European results. The team has participated in UEFA Champions League, Europa League, and Conference League qualifying rounds, including landmark achievements such as advancing deeper into qualifiers than any previous Armenian club and reaching the UEFA Conference League group stage. Today, Pyunik remains one of the most decorated and influential clubs in Armenian football history.

name: FC Shirak
content:
FC Shirak is a professional football club based in Gyumri, Armenia, and is one of the oldest and most traditional clubs in the country. The club was originally founded in 1958 during the Soviet era and was known as Shirak Leninakan, later changing its name after the city of Leninakan was renamed Gyumri. Shirak has played a major role in Armenian football since the formation of the national league following Armenia’s independence in 1992.

The club is one of the founding members of the Armenian Premier League and has a strong domestic history. Shirak was among the first champions of the league in 1992, sharing the title with Pyunik after both teams finished level on points. Over the years, the club has remained one of the most competitive sides in Armenia, winning multiple league titles, Armenian Cups, and Super Cups, and consistently competing in top positions of the domestic competition.

Shirak is also known for its strong identity and loyal fanbase, whose supporters are called the “Black Panthers.” The club plays its home matches at Gyumri City Stadium, one of the oldest stadiums in Armenia. Beyond domestic success, Shirak has regularly participated in UEFA competition qualifiers, representing Armenian football in Europe and contributing to the development of players through its youth academy.

name: FC Alashkert
content:
FC Alashkert is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. Founded in 1990 in the town of Martuni, the club experienced an early period of instability and was dissolved in 2000 before being re-established in 2011. Since its revival, Alashkert has become a regular participant in the Armenian Premier League, playing its home matches at the Alashkert Stadium in the capital.

The club quickly rose to prominence in Armenian football during the 2010s, winning the Armenian Premier League title four times and securing additional domestic trophies, including the Armenian Cup and multiple Supercups. Their success was largely driven by strong investment and management, as well as a period of consistent performances that established them as one of the leading teams in the country.

On the international stage, Alashkert made history in 2021 by becoming the first Armenian club to reach the group stage of a UEFA-organized competition, qualifying for the UEFA Europa Conference League. This achievement marked a significant milestone not only for the club but also for Armenian football, highlighting Alashkert’s growing competitiveness in European competitions.

name: FC Urartu
content:
FC Urartu is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia, founded on 20 January 1992. The club was originally known as FC Banants and was established in the Kotayk region before later relocating and becoming one of the key teams in the Armenian Premier League. In 2019, the club officially rebranded as FC Urartu, taking its name from the ancient kingdom of Urartu, which is an important historical reference in Armenian culture.

Since its early years, Urartu has developed into a competitive force in Armenian football. The club won its first major trophy shortly after its founding by claiming the Armenian Cup in 1992, and it has since added multiple domestic titles, including league championships in 2013–14 and 2022–23. Urartu has also frequently competed for top positions in the Armenian Premier League and has been a consistent participant in national cup competitions.

On the European stage, FC Urartu has represented Armenia in UEFA competitions, including the Champions League, Europa League, and Conference League qualifiers. While the club has not yet reached the group stages of the Champions League, it has gained valuable international experience and contributed to raising the profile of Armenian club football. Today, Urartu is considered one of the strongest and most professionally structured clubs in Armenia, with its own stadium and training academy in Yerevan.

name: FC Ararat-Armenia
content:
FC Ararat-Armenia is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia, founded in 2017. The club was originally established as FC Avan Academy, built around young players from the Yerevan Football Academy and several players from FC Pyunik. It quickly entered competitive football and played its first official matches in the Armenian First League before rapidly progressing to the top division.

In 2018, the club underwent major changes when it was taken over by Russian-Armenian businessman Samvel Karapetyan. During this period, it briefly carried the name Ararat-Moskva before being restructured and renamed FC Ararat-Armenia after gaining entry into the Armenian Premier League. The club quickly became one of the strongest teams in the country, achieving immediate success at the top level.

Ararat-Armenia won its first Armenian Premier League title in the 2018–19 season and successfully defended it in 2019–20, establishing itself as a dominant force in domestic football. The club has also competed in UEFA competitions, including the Champions League and Europa League qualifiers, where it earned notable wins in early rounds. Today, Ararat-Armenia is considered one of the leading modern clubs in Armenian football, known for its rapid rise and strong competitive structure.

name: Araks Ararat FC
content:
Araks Ararat FC is an Armenian football club based in the town of Ararat in the Ararat Province. The club was originally founded in 1960 as FC Ararat, representing the local community, and later changed its name several times due to ownership and structural changes, including FC Tsement Ararat in 1993 and eventually Araks Ararat in 2000. The club’s identity is closely tied to the industrial and regional history of the Ararat area.

During the late 1990s, Araks Ararat became one of the strongest teams in Armenian football. Under the name FC Tsement Ararat, the club achieved major success, winning the Armenian Premier League in 1998 and again in 2000 under the Araks name. It also won multiple domestic cups during this period, establishing itself as a top contender in Armenian football at the time.

Despite its success, the club faced financial difficulties and organizational instability in the early 2000s. It was eventually dissolved in 2003 after being relocated and restructured, though there were later attempts to revive the team in lower divisions and amateur competitions. Today, Araks Ararat is remembered as one of the historically successful but now defunct clubs in Armenian football history.

name: FC Ararat Yerevan
content:
FC Ararat Yerevan is one of the oldest and most historic football clubs in Armenia, based in the capital city Yerevan. The club was founded in 1935 and became especially prominent during the Soviet era, when it competed under the Armenian SSR system. Its nickname is “The White Eagles,” and it plays its home matches at the Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium, one of the main stadiums in Armenia. The club has long been considered a symbol of Armenian football tradition.

The greatest period in Ararat Yerevan’s history came in the 1970s. In 1973, the club achieved its most famous success by winning the Soviet Top League and the Soviet Cup in the same season, becoming one of the strongest teams in the USSR at the time. This achievement made Ararat Yerevan internationally recognized and remains the club’s most iconic sporting accomplishment. It also established the team as a historic name in post-Soviet Armenian football culture.

After the collapse of the Soviet Union, Ararat Yerevan continued competing in the Armenian Premier League, where it became one of the most popular and traditional clubs in the country. While the club has not dominated consistently like some modern Armenian teams, it has still won domestic trophies and remains highly respected for its legacy, fan base, and contribution to Armenian football development. Today, Ararat Yerevan is seen as a historic institution rather than a modern powerhouse, but it still plays an important role in the national league.

name: FC Noah
content:
FC Noah is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia, founded in 2017. The club originally started under the name FC Artsakh before being rebranded as FC Noah in 2019. The new name is inspired by the biblical figure Noah and the symbolic connection to Mount Ararat, which is an important national symbol for Armenia. Despite being a very young club, Noah quickly established itself in Armenian football and moved up from the First League to the Premier League within its first season.

The club’s first major success came in the 2019–20 season, when it won the Armenian Cup after a dramatic final against Ararat-Armenia, marking its first major trophy. This victory also secured its place in European competitions, allowing Noah to represent Armenia in UEFA tournaments. Since then, the club has continued to grow rapidly, strengthening its squad and infrastructure while competing regularly in the Armenian Premier League.

In recent years, FC Noah has become one of the most ambitious clubs in Armenia. The team achieved a historic milestone in 2024 by reaching the UEFA Europa Conference League league phase after progressing through all qualifying rounds, defeating several strong European opponents along the way. More recently, Noah also won the Armenian league and cup double, confirming its status as one of the leading modern forces in Armenian football.

name: Ulisses FC
content:
Ulisses FC was a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. The club was founded in 2000, originally under the name Dinamo-2000 Yerevan, and it quickly entered the Armenian football system by competing in the top division. Over time, the club changed its identity due to sponsorship and ownership changes, becoming Dinamo-Zenit Yerevan in 2004 and finally adopting the name Ulisses FC in 2006.

The club played in the Armenian Premier League and experienced both mid-table seasons and a period of major success. Its greatest achievement came in 2011, when Ulisses FC won the Armenian Premier League title for the first time, breaking the long dominance of other leading clubs in the country. This victory marked the peak of the club’s history and earned it a place in UEFA competitions, where it represented Armenia in European qualifiers.

Despite this success, Ulisses FC struggled with financial and organizational stability in later years. The club gradually declined and finished last in the 2015–16 season before ultimately withdrawing from the league and being dissolved in 2016. Today, Ulisses is remembered as a short-lived but notable Armenian club that briefly reached the top of national football before disappearing.

name: FC Yerevan
content:
FC Yerevan is a defunct Armenian football club based in Yerevan. The club was founded in 1995 and quickly rose into the Armenian football system, becoming part of the Armenian Premier League. It played its home matches at Hrazdan Stadium and competed during the early years of Armenia’s post-independence football structure.

The club achieved its greatest success in 1997, when it won the Armenian Premier League title. Despite this achievement, FC Yerevan had a relatively short lifespan at the top level, and it struggled to maintain long-term stability compared to more established clubs in the country. It remained active in the top division for a few seasons but could not sustain consistent performance or financial security.

After the 1999 season, FC Yerevan was dissolved, and its players moved on to other clubs in the capital. Although the club was short-lived, it is remembered as part of the early development period of Armenian professional football in the 1990s, when the league was still forming its structure and identity.

name: SC Mika
content:
FC Mika is a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. It was founded in 1998 as Mika-Kasakh Ashtarak in the town of Ashtarak and was established by the Mika Corporation LLC owned by Armenian-Russian businessman Mikhail Baghdasarov. The club later moved to Yerevan in 2007, where it became more closely associated with its own stadium and the capital’s football infrastructure.

The club achieved early success in Armenian football, winning its first major trophy—the Armenian Cup—in 2000. During the 2000s, FC Mika became one of the competitive sides in the Armenian Premier League and regularly fought for domestic cups, adding multiple Armenian Cup titles and Supercups to its collection. Its most successful period was the mid-to-late 2000s and early 2010s, when it was consistently among the stronger teams in the league.

However, FC Mika faced financial and organizational difficulties, and by the end of the 2015–16 season the club withdrew from professional football. It later returned in 2022, re-entering the Armenian First League and continuing to compete at a lower level. Today, FC Mika is seen as a club with a history of domestic success but also periods of instability and rebuilding.

name: FC Gandzasar
content:
FC Gandzasar Kapan is a professional football club based in the town of Kapan in the Syunik Province of Armenia. The club was founded in 2004 and is closely connected to the local mining industry, being owned by the Zangezur Copper and Molybdenum Combine. It plays its home matches at the Kapan Stadium, which has a capacity of around 3,000–3,500 spectators and is one of the main sports venues in southern Armenia.

Throughout its history, Gandzasar Kapan has moved between divisions of Armenian football, competing in both the Armenian Premier League and the First League. The club’s strongest period came in the 2010s, when it consistently finished in the upper part of the table and even achieved a runner-up finish in the league in the 2016–17 season. It also reached the Armenian Cup final in 2013–14, showing its competitiveness at domestic level.

On the European stage, Gandzasar Kapan has represented Armenia in UEFA Europa League qualifying rounds several times. Although the club has not advanced far in these competitions, it has gained experience against stronger European teams, contributing to its development and visibility. Today, Gandzasar is considered a solid but fluctuating club in Armenian football, with periods of both top-flight presence and relegation battles.

name: Zvartnots-AAL FC
content:
Zvartnots-AAL FC was a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. It was founded in February 1997 by Armenian Airlines, the country’s former state-owned national carrier, and was originally created to represent the airline in domestic football. The club entered the Armenian First League in its debut year and quickly established itself as a competitive side, earning promotion to the top division after winning the First League in 1998.

After reaching the Armenian Premier League, Zvartnots-AAL developed into a strong contender in the early 2000s. The club achieved notable success by finishing as league runners-up in 2001 and reaching the Armenian Cup finals twice (2000 and 2002), although it never managed to win a major domestic trophy. During this period, it was considered one of the more ambitious clubs in Armenian football and even secured European qualification.

The club also took part in European competition, appearing in the UEFA Cup during the 2002–03 season, where it was eliminated in the qualifying rounds. However, financial issues and failure to meet league requirements led to its withdrawal from the Armenian Premier League in 2003, after which the club was dissolved.

name: Erebuni-Homenmen FC
content:
Erebuni-Homenmen FC was a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. The club was founded in 1992, shortly after Armenia’s independence, under the name Homenmen-FIMA FC. It was initially linked to the Armenian State Institute of Physical Culture and participated in the first Armenian football competitions after the collapse of the Soviet Union. Over the next few years, the club went through several name changes, becoming Homenmen Yerevan and later Erebuni-Homenmen FC in 1997.

The club competed in the Armenian Premier League throughout the 1990s and remained a stable top-flight side without ever being relegated. However, it struggled with financial and organizational instability, especially after losing support from the Homenmen organization in 1999. Despite these issues, Erebuni-Homenmen still managed to participate in early UEFA competitions such as the Intertoto Cup, representing Armenian football in Europe on a limited scale.

In 2000, the club was ultimately dissolved due to financial difficulties, marking the end of its short existence in professional football. Although it did not achieve major trophies, Erebuni-Homenmen is remembered as one of the early post-Soviet Armenian clubs that helped shape the country’s domestic league during its formative years.

name: Spartak Yerevan FC
content:
Spartak Yerevan FC was a short-lived Armenian football club based in Yerevan. It was founded in 2000 under the name FC Araks-Impeks, after taking over the league license of the financially troubled Araks Ararat FC, allowing it to compete in the Armenian Premier League. During the 2001 season, the club changed its name to Spartak Yerevan and continued playing in the top division.

The club competed in the Armenian Premier League in the early 2000s and also participated in European competitions. It played in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers in the 2001–02 season and in the UEFA Cup qualifiers in 2002–03, although it was eliminated in the early rounds in both cases.

After the 2002 season, the club was dissolved, and its players were transferred mainly to FC Banants (now FC Urartu). Despite its very short existence, Spartak Yerevan is remembered as one of several transitional clubs in early 2000s Armenian football, formed during a period of frequent financial instability and restructuring in the league.

name: FC Kotayk
content:
FC Kotayk was a professional football club based in Abovyan, Kotayk Province, Armenia. The club was founded in 1955, making it one of the oldest football teams in the country. During the Soviet era, Kotayk competed in various USSR league divisions and was considered a stable regional team representing the Kotayk region.

After Armenia gained independence, FC Kotayk became a founding participant of the Armenian Premier League in 1992. The club had periods of competitiveness in the 1990s and early 2000s, and it also took part in European competitions such as the UEFA Cup Winners’ Cup and the UEFA Intertoto Cup, gaining limited but notable international experience. However, despite some strong seasons, the club never became a consistent title contender.

FC Kotayk struggled financially in the 2000s, which eventually led to its dissolution in 2005. There were later attempts to revive the club, including a short return in 2016, but these efforts were unsuccessful due to ongoing financial issues, and the team withdrew again. Today, FC Kotayk is remembered as one of Armenia’s historic clubs that played an important role in both Soviet and early post-independence football.

name: Kilikia FC
content:
FC Kilikia was a professional football club based in Yerevan, Armenia. The club was originally founded in 1992 and was part of the first season of the Armenian football championship after the country gained independence. It initially competed in the Armenian Premier League and played its home matches at the Hrazdan Stadium, one of the largest stadiums in Armenia.

In its early years, Kilikia went through instability, including a merger with FC Malatia and later separation, before being disbanded for a short period. The club was later revived in 1997 and re-entered the Armenian football system. In the following years, it fluctuated between divisions and even replaced other financially struggling clubs in the Premier League. Although it was never a dominant force, it occasionally achieved solid mid-table finishes and qualified for European competition, including the UEFA Intertoto Cup.

FC Kilikia struggled with long-term financial stability and eventually could not sustain participation at the top level. In 2011, the club officially withdrew from the Armenian Premier League due to financial problems and was subsequently dissolved. After a long absence, it later returned in amateur competitions in the 2020s, but it no longer operates as a top-tier professional club.

name: Impuls FC
content:
Impuls FC was a professional football club based in Dilijan, Tavush Province, Armenia. The club was originally founded in 1985 during the Soviet era and went through several periods of inactivity and revival. After Armenia’s independence, it was re-established and eventually returned to professional competition, becoming part of the Armenian football system. The team played its home matches at the Dilijan City Stadium, which was also upgraded during the club’s time in the top division.

The club’s modern era began in 2009, when Impuls FC was revived and entered the Armenian First League, quickly earning promotion to the Armenian Premier League. During this period, the team became a solid mid-table competitor and reached one of its peak achievements in 2012, when it finished as runner-up in the Armenian Cup. This period marked the strongest phase in the club’s short modern history, as it consistently competed against Armenia’s leading teams.

Despite its competitive performances, Impuls FC struggled with financial stability, which ultimately led to its dissolution in May 2013. After the club was disbanded, it ceased all professional activities, and its stadium and facilities were later used mainly for youth football development in the region. Today, Impuls FC is remembered as a short-lived but competitive Armenian club that briefly reached the top level of national football.

name: Lori FC
content:
Lori FC is a professional football club based in Vanadzor, Armenia. It was originally founded in 1936 and has a long history that dates back to the Soviet era, when it competed in the USSR football league system. After Armenia’s independence, Lori became one of the founding participants of the Armenian Premier League in 1992, although the club frequently moved between divisions during its early post-independence years.

During the 1990s and early 2000s, Lori FC experienced a “yo-yo” period of promotions and relegations. The club spent time in both the Armenian Premier League and the First League, but struggled with consistency and financial stability. It played its last top-flight seasons in the early 2000s before being dissolved in 2005.

The club was revived in 2017 by local businessman Tovmas Grigoryan and quickly returned to professional football, winning the Armenian First League in the 2017–18 season and earning promotion to the Premier League. However, after several seasons, Lori faced administrative and financial difficulties again and withdrew from the Armenian Premier League in 2021, effectively ending its professional activity.

name: Noravank SC
content:
Noravank SC is a very short-lived Armenian football club, but it has a pretty interesting story for how quickly it rose and disappeared.

The club was founded in 2020 in Vayk (Vayots Dzor region) and started in the Armenian First League. Despite being a brand-new team, it managed to perform well enough to earn promotion to the Armenian Premier League in 2021, which is already unusual for such a young club.

Even more surprisingly, in the 2021–22 season, Noravank achieved its biggest success: it won the Armenian Cup, beating FC Urartu in the final. That victory gave the club its first major trophy and a potential place in European competitions, which is rare for a team in its first top-flight season. However, the club’s success didn’t last. Noravank struggled with licensing and financial stability issues, and after the 2021–22 season it was dissolved in 2022, just two years after being founded. Today, it is remembered as one of the most extreme “rise and fall” stories in modern Armenian football — a club that won a national trophy almost immediately, then disappeared just as fast.
